dialogue_text: You see, Oz is a Great Wizard, and can take on any form he wishes. So that some say he looks like a bird; and some say he looks like an elephant; and some say he looks like a cat. To others he appears as a beautiful fairy, or a brownie, or in any other form that pleases him. But who the real Oz is, when he is in his own form, no living person can tell.
source_excerpt: "That is hard to tell," said the man thoughtfully. "You see, Oz is a Great Wizard, and can take on any form he wishes. So that some say he looks like a bird; and some say he looks like an elephant; and some say he looks like a cat. To ot...

speaker.notes: Attributed through a role label rather than a canonical name.

source_excerpt: "That is very strange," said Dorothy, "but we must try, in some way, to see him, or we shall have made our journey for nothing."
